title: Add compounding-knowledge eval scenarios
description: Extend eval guidance to cover source ingest, synthesis updates, query-to-note filing, contradiction linting, stale-source detection, and orphan/missing-link checks.
acceptance_criteria: docs/evals/baseline-scenarios.md and docs/evals/agent-production.md include scenarios that test whether knowledge compounds instead of being rediscovered from scratch.
close_reason: Implemented LLM Wiki alignment docs, skill guidance, and compounding-knowledge eval scenarios; pending manual review before commit/push.

title: Document LLM Wiki alignment in OpenClerk vision
description: Update the research note, README, architecture doc, and OpenClerk skill docs to describe how Karpathy's LLM Wiki pattern maps onto OpenClerk's agent-first knowledge plane.
acceptance_criteria: Research note includes a comparison section; README and architecture docs describe LLM-maintained synthesis; skill guidance tells agents when to search, ingest, synthesize, and inspect provenance.
close_reason: Implemented LLM Wiki alignment docs, skill guidance, and compounding-knowledge eval scenarios; pending manual review before commit/push.

title: Tighten embedded Go install story for v0.1.0
description: Update the public install story so OpenClerk is documented as an embedded Go module first. Rewrite the top-level install and quick-start docs around client/local, align maintainer and skill docs with the same contract, add authored package docs for client/local and client/openclerk, and keep the release contract source-only for the first public v0.1.0 tag.
acceptance_criteria: README, SKILL, CONTRIBUTING, and maintainer docs all describe one canonical install command for client/local at v0.1.0; authored package docs reinforce embedded-first usage; external smoke test installs client/local@v0.1.0; go test ./... stays green.
notes: Implemented the embedded install-story pass locally: README, SKILL, CONTRIBUTING, and maintainer docs now use a single client/local@v0.1.0 install command; added authored package docs for client/local and client/openclerk; updated the external smoke test to install client/local@v0.1.0. Validation: gofmt check passed, focused embedded smoke passed, example run passed, go test ./... passed, go generate ./... produced no tracked changes. golangci-lint was unavailable in the local environment.

title: Fix provenance drift in sync and projection rebuilds
description: Address the review findings around no-op startup syncs mutating updated_at, records invalidation for plain notes, and graph/records projection refresh events being emitted for unaffected refs.
close_reason: Closed

title: Prototype the first promoted-domain model beyond generic entity extraction
description: Replace or extend the current entity_* plus Facts projection with an explicit promoted-domain baseline that has stable identifiers, update semantics, provenance hooks, and tests proving where structured state beats plain documents.

title: Promote provenance_events into truth-sync v1
description: Turn the existing provenance ledger into a real truth-sync substrate with standard cross-store refs, richer event types, projection status and freshness fields, and read APIs for inspecting derivation, invalidation, and current authority.
close_reason: Implemented in the unified knowledge-plane rewrite: added the public openclerk client and embedded runtime entrypoint, rewrote public framing and examples, expanded the docs service and provenance APIs, and added baseline eval scenarios and tests.

title: Close the docs-service capability gaps
description: Add first-class docs-service capabilities from the architecture note: document listing over stable ids and vault-relative paths, metadata and link-aware retrieval surfaces, and inspectable provenance needed for agent-facing repair and source-grounded answers.
close_reason: Implemented in the unified knowledge-plane rewrite: added the public openclerk client and embedded runtime entrypoint, rewrote public framing and examples, expanded the docs service and provenance APIs, and added baseline eval scenarios and tests.

title: Collapse the public API and SDK to a single OpenClerk surface
description: Generate and adopt one public client from the canonical OpenAPI contract, move backend selection behind the embedded runtime and eval workflow, and stop presenting FTS/hybrid/graph/records as long-term user-facing package choices.
close_reason: Implemented in the unified knowledge-plane rewrite: added the public openclerk client and embedded runtime entrypoint, rewrote public framing and examples, expanded the docs service and provenance APIs, and added baseline eval scenarios and tests.

title: Add eval harness and baseline capture for implementation variants
description: Build an eval task set for docs retrieval, structured lookup, provenance, freshness, and update correctness so FTS, hybrid, graph, and promoted-domain projections can be compared as internal implementation variants behind one public product surface.
close_reason: Implemented in the unified knowledge-plane rewrite: added the public openclerk client and embedded runtime entrypoint, rewrote public framing and examples, expanded the docs service and provenance APIs, and added baseline eval scenarios and tests.

title: Rewrite public framing around the agent knowledge plane
description: Replace health-record and multi-backend product framing in README, SKILL, examples, and user-visible tests. Present OpenClerk as a local-first agent-facing notes/document/record store with canonical docs, optional derived graph, optional promoted domains, and provenance-backed retrieval.
close_reason: Implemented in the unified knowledge-plane rewrite: added the public openclerk client and embedded runtime entrypoint, rewrote public framing and examples, expanded the docs service and provenance APIs, and added baseline eval scenarios and tests.

title: Write the knowledge-plane realignment spec
description: Capture the concrete target model for the single public OpenClerk surface, define the role of docs/graph/records/provenance, record the deprecation posture for backend-specific public packages, and align repo docs and implementation decisions with the agent-first knowledge-plane architecture note.
close_reason: Implemented in the unified knowledge-plane rewrite: added the public openclerk client and embedded runtime entrypoint, rewrote public framing and examples, expanded the docs service and provenance APIs, and added baseline eval scenarios and tests.

title: Realign OpenClerk as an agent-first knowledge plane
description: Reposition OpenClerk from a multi-backend PoC framing to a single-surface agent-facing knowledge plane. Scope includes public framing, single-client/public-contract migration, docs-service capability gaps, provenance/truth-sync promotion, eval-driven implementation selection, and archival of backend-specific public surfaces once benchmarks exist.
design: Karpathy's LLM Wiki pattern is related to OpenClerk and should be incorporated as source-linked synthesis within the docs layer. OpenClerk should not clone the gist literally; it should make the pattern auditable through stable ids, citations, provenance events, projection freshness, and evals while preserving canonical docs, selective promoted records, and deferred memory/routing.

title: Unblock PR #1 dependency review and rewrite clean merge branch
description:
Why this issue exists and what needs to be done:
- enable the repository setting required for GitHub dependency review so PR #1 can pass all checks
- rewrite PR #1 so it contains only the agent-store work and excludes the unrelated bd init commit d36168b
- verify the rewritten branch locally and in GitHub Actions
- prepare the repository for squash merge, sync, and branch cleanup under the session-close workflow
notes: 2026-04-15: Enabled GitHub dependabot security updates on yazanabuashour/openclerk, rebuilt PR #1 on a clean branch from origin/main as local branch codex/agent-store-pocs-clean at 94896b3, and verified locally with go generate ./..., git diff --exit-code, gofmt -l on tracked Go files, and go test ./.... Pending manual review before any push, PR branch force-update, GitHub Actions rerun, merge, fetch --prune, pull, or local branch deletion.
close_reason: Completed via PR #1 rewrite, CI unblock, and squash merge

title: Address agent-store review findings
description:
Fix the four review findings from the new agent-store PoC patch.

Scope:
- reject duplicate document paths on create
- prune deleted vault files from sqlite state during startup sync
- preserve the caller-supplied title on create
- attach graph link edges to the emitting chunk node so chunk neighborhoods surface outgoing links
close_reason: Completed via PR #1 rewrite, CI unblock, and squash merge

title: Implement FTS PoC backend
description: Implement deterministic document registry, deterministic chunking, SQLite FTS5 search, citation-bearing retrieval, and safe document writes for the fts backend.
close_reason: Closed

title: Implement hybrid PoC backend
description: Implement hybrid lexical plus embedding search in SQLite with persisted embeddings, reciprocal-rank fusion, and lexical-only fallback when no embedding provider is configured.
close_reason: Closed

title: Implement graph PoC backend
description: Implement derived graph tables and the graph extension endpoint with evidence-linked neighbors and canonical doc/chunk provenance.
close_reason: Closed

title: Implement records PoC backend
description: Implement promoted entity tables, provenance ledger behavior, records lookup and entity endpoints, and invalidation on canonical document updates.
close_reason: Closed

title: Add shared contract, generation, and daemon runtime
description: Author the v1 OpenAPI contract, generation pipeline, generated Go client packages, shared daemon entrypoint, strict internal interfaces, and contract/runtime parity checks.
close_reason: Closed

title: Implement agent-store Go PoC clients
description:
Build local-first SQLite-backed OpenClerk document-store PoCs with one OpenAPI-first contract and committed generated Go clients.

Scope:
- shared OpenAPI contract and generated Go SDK packages
- shared HTTP daemon and strict internal boundaries
- four backends: fts, hybrid, graph, records
- install-focused examples and verification

Out of scope:
- protobuf/Connect transport
- auth/users
- eval harnesses
acceptance_criteria:
- one generated Go client package per PoC is committed
- one shared HTTP daemon serves all PoCs
- SQLite is the only persisted store
- docs are canonical and extension endpoints stay namespaced by PoC
close_reason: Closed

title: Prototype source-linked synthesis workflow
description: Prototype the LLM Wiki workflow using existing OpenClerk docs/search/provenance APIs before adding new SDK or OpenAPI surface.
acceptance_criteria: Agents can create/update synthesis pages with source refs and freshness metadata; prototype records provenance; no new public API is added unless evals show the current surface is insufficient.

title: Add code-first local OpenClerk SDK facade
description: Implement a hand-written client/local facade for routine OpenClerk agent workflows, update production skill/docs/examples to prefer it, and add focused tests matching the approved plan.
close_reason: Implemented code-first local OpenClerk SDK facade, docs, examples, skill guidance, eval plan, and tests.

title: Update manual review workflow
description: Revise AGENTS.md session completion instructions so agents stop for manual review before staging, committing, or pushing, and only commit/push after explicit approval.
close_reason: Updated AGENTS.md manual review workflow to pause before staging, committing, or pushing.

title: Improve OpenClerk skill usability
description: Update the OpenClerk agent skill and reference docs with common local query guidance, and add a narrow runnable openclerk-query example for inspecting local documents, search, records, links, and provenance without changing SDK or OpenAPI surfaces.
close_reason: Implemented OpenClerk skill common-query guidance and openclerk-query example; verified formatting, helper smoke tests, and go test ./....

title: Repackage OpenClerk skill as an Agent Skills package
description: Create a canonical spec-compliant skill directory at skills/openclerk, move the existing agent guidance into a frontmatter-based SKILL.md, update repo references and CI from the repo-root SKILL.md to the new package, and add validator-based CI coverage for the Agent Skills spec.
close_reason: Closed

title: Archive backend-specific public packages after single-surface adoption
description: Once the single public client and eval harness are in place, remove or archive backend-specific public packages and examples so implementation diversity lives behind evals instead of the primary product surface.

title: Decide whether to keep the separate Beads bootstrap branch
description:
Why this issue exists and what needs to be done:
- commit d36168b (bd init: initialize beads issue tracking) was intentionally kept out of PR #1
- the commit is preserved on remote branch codex/beads-init for follow-up review
- decide whether to open a dedicated PR, adapt the bootstrap to the current repository state, or drop it entirely
notes: Decision: drop codex/beads-init instead of merging it. The branch's only unique commit d36168b adds agent/bootstrap-specific files (CLAUDE.md, .claude/settings.json), switches .beads/metadata.json away from the current tracked configuration, and includes embedded Dolt runtime artifacts (.beads/embeddeddolt/**). Current main already has the tracked Beads project files and AGENTS.md instructions, so merging this branch would add stale or machine-specific bootstrap state without improving the project.
close_reason: Dropped obsolete codex/beads-init branch after review; no tracked repo changes required

title: Document install flow and ship generated Go clients
description: Add README install guidance, per-client examples, and smoke coverage for consuming the generated Go packages from an external module.
close_reason: Closed

title: Initialize Go module and refresh local tooling
description: Why: the repository does not yet have a runnable Go package or Go toolchain setup, so maintainers cannot build or test application code. What: initialize the repository as a Go module, add a minimal runnable package, and update local tool version management so Go-related tooling is pinned to current versions alongside the existing maintainer tools.
close_reason: Closed

title: Open-source readiness backlog
description: Track readiness items that should not be implemented until the repository grows beyond the current scaffold stage. This issue groups deferred work around real runtime checks, stronger release integrity, deployment trust boundaries, maintainer isolation, and ongoing security operations.

title: Harden releases when the first distributable artifact exists
description: Once the project ships a distributable artifact, strengthen release authenticity and supply-chain evidence. This includes signed tags or artifacts, provenance, SBOM publication, checksums, and making the build reproducible enough to verify release integrity.
acceptance_criteria: Release process includes signing or attestations appropriate to the artifact type, publishes an SBOM and checksums when relevant, and documents how users can verify release integrity.
close_reason: Closed

title: Add real contributor gates when the first runnable package or app lands
description: When the repository gains its first runnable package or application, add truthful contributor gates tied to that surface. This includes real test commands, local run instructions, compatibility expectations, support policy details, and language-appropriate code scanning or linting.
acceptance_criteria: Contributor docs include real setup and validation commands; CI enforces the actual checks needed for the first runnable component; support and compatibility expectations match the shipped surface.
close_reason: Closed

title: Add deployment and publishing trust boundaries when deploys exist
description: If the project introduces deployment targets or package publishing, establish separate trust boundaries for build, test, and release. Move to short-lived OIDC credentials, protected environments, and tight workflow permissions for tags, releases, packages, and deployments.
acceptance_criteria: Release and deploy workflows use protected environments where applicable, avoid long-lived secrets, and scope write permissions only to the workflows that need them.
notes: Workflow changes are in place: release.yml now uses the release environment, OIDC-backed GitHub attestations, tight workflow permissions, and no long-lived secrets. Remaining maintainer action is to protect the release environment and v* tags in GitHub settings before the first public release.

title: Raise maintainer isolation as automation grows
description: As repository automation expands, reduce maintainer and bot blast radius. This includes dedicated low-privilege bot identities, stronger first-time contributor approval rules if warranted, self-hosted runner policy, and stricter review enforcement once more than one maintainer can satisfy it.
acceptance_criteria: Maintainer and automation identities follow least privilege, untrusted PR execution policy is documented, and branch/review enforcement matches the actual maintainer model without making the repo unworkable.

title: Establish ongoing security operations
description: Move beyond one-time security setup once the project has meaningful runtime or release surfaces. This includes a recurring dependency review cadence, advisory workflow rehearsal, and threat modeling, abuse-case testing, or fuzzing for sensitive functionality.
acceptance_criteria: Maintainers have a documented recurring security review cadence and, where relevant, a deeper testing plan for high-risk project surfaces.
